column8: Mother saw that Nick (not to wash) his hands.
column9: The teacher (to understand) that Lena hadn't done her homework.
column10: I knew that my friend (not to come) yet.
column11: When I (to wake) up yesterday, father had already gone to work.
column12: Nick thought that his father (not to come) home yet.
column13: Mary (to tell) us that she had cooked a good dinner.
column14: Yesterday I found the book which I (to lose) in summer.
column15: When we (to come) to the station, the train had already left.
column16: By two o’clock the teacher (to examine) all the students.
column17: On my way to school I (to remember) that I had left my report at home.
column18: My friends were glad to hear that I (to pass) all the exams successfully.
column19: Poor Oliver (to lie) unconscious on the spot where Sikes had left him.
